allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking supports elasticity by helping teams see where boundaries can stretch without losing the larger operating picture.
allies.source_quote: systems thinking provides the framework for seeing interrelationships and patterns of change

allies.rationale_text: Abstraction helps elasticity compress what matters most when the system must fit hard cognitive or context constraints.
allies.source_quote: find a way to dynamically extract and present only the most relevant data

antagonists.rationale_text: Status-quo-bias weakens elasticity when fixed roles and familiar boundaries are protected even after the environment changes.
antagonists.source_quote: fixed skills are punished, while elastic skills are prized

structured_tensions.tension_text: Elasticity conflicts with narratives when story-led demand assumptions replace observed response curves.
structured_tensions.source_quote: elasticity vs narratives: story-led demand assumptions replace observed response curves.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Elasticity conflicts with optionality when excessive pricing or pathway branching erodes the signal quality needed for slope estimation.
structured_tensions.source_quote: elasticity vs optionality: excessive pricing branches erode signal quality needed for slope estimation.

curation_notes.summary: Relation surface stays inside the current graph vocabulary and keeps omission stronger than weak forcing.
donor_drops: Did not widen Wave 3 target ids or import donor-shaped relation neighborhoods to rescue weak local grounding.
open_questions: Some source-real neighbors remain omitted because the current graph vocabulary would force weaker mappings than this conservative pass allows.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only and is noted only in batch artifacts.
antagonists: No multihop or higher-order semantics were introduced in these Wave 3 files.
structured_tensions: Explicit tensions were preserved only where the raw source clearly earned a current-vocabulary target.
